title: DTC Explanation: P0300, P0301, P0302, P0303

blocks.text: DTC Explanation: P0300, P0301, P0302, P0303
DTC P0300: Multiple cylinder fire DTC P0301: No.1 cylinder misfire DTC P0302: No.2 cylinder misfire DTC P0303: No.3 cylinder misfire

When ignition occurs in each cylinder during engine operation, the crankshaft rotation speed fluctuates slightly. The PGM-FI ECU monitors the crankshaft rotation speed based on the pulse signal output from the crank sensor. The PGM-FI ECU counts the number of misfires and determines the cylinder in which a misfire occurs by monitoring the change in the crankshaft rotation speed.
There are two types of fire loss determination

items.text: Type 1: When the number of ignition occurrences per 200 rpm reaches a level that causes the catalytic converter to burn out, the DTC is stored and the PGM-FI warning light flashes. If no more ignition occurs, the PGM-FI warning light changes from flashing to on.

Type 2: If the number of ignition occurrences per 1,000 RPM is at a level that affects the exhaust gases and does not lead to catalytic converter burnout, the DTC is stored and the PGM-FI warning light is illuminated.

blocks.text: Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status

rows.text: 24-hour (e.g. observation, operation (of a machine), care (of patient))
Detection order (of the DTC in question)
2D/C (2 consecutive detection method), PGM-FI warning light: On
Normal judgment, failure judgment, not completed, out of condition
Elapsed time since end of monitoring (except immediately after engine start)
engine Inhalation air pressure [Intake pressure Sensor]*
DTC requesting the prohibition of detection of the relevant DTC
To prevent misjudgment, test runs should be conducted on level ground.

When the engine coolant temperature [water temperature sensor] at engine startup is -10°C or lower, the failure detection is executed when the engine coolant temperature [water temperature sensor] is 20°C or higher.

blocks.text: If the number of misfire occurrences relative to engine speed is greater than or equal to the following table.

items.text: Start the engine, run the radiator fan twice, and then warm up the engine for at least 2 minutes at 3,000 rpm with no load.
Vehicle speed [vehicle speed] 25-100 km/h for at least 3 minutes.
Stop the vehicle and let it idle for at least 3 minutes.
If freeze data has been stored, a test run shall be performed under conditions similar to the freeze data instead of steps 2 and 3 of the above test run.
If the road or traffic conditions make it difficult to reproduce, the test run should be repeated several times.
When conducting test drives, the driver must comply with all laws and regulations, observe good manners, and drive in accordance with the surrounding conditions.

blocks.text: Type 1 fire (catalytic converter is burned out and purification performance is expected to deteriorate to an extreme degree)

items.text: If the vehicle detects a type 1 misfire at the first D/C, the PGM-FI warning light flashes and a temporary DTC is stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory. At this time, the fuel injection stop function is activated for the cylinder where the abnormality is detected. This function stops fuel injection for the cylinder in which a misfire occurs at high engine speed. The PGM-FI warning light continues to flash until the same D/C operation is completed, and the fuel injection stop function continues to operate.

If the same failure is detected at the next D/C (two consecutive detections), the PGM-FI warning light illuminates and the DTC and freeze data are stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory, along with activation of the fuel injection stop function for the cylinder in which the misfire occurred.

After the same failure is detected for 2 D/C (2 consecutive detections), from the next D/C (3 D/C) onward, the PGM-FI warning light will remain on until clearing*** the temporary DTC in the PGM-FI ECU memory is performed. However, if a misfire is detected in this state, the PGM-FI warning light will shift from lighting to flashing.

blocks.text: Type 2 ignition (ignition that does not result in catalytic converter burnout but is expected to degrade purification performance)

items.text: If the PGM-FI ECU detects a type 2 misfire at the first D/C, the PGM-FI warning light is not turned on and flashes, and a temporary DTC is stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory. If the same fault is detected at the next and subsequent DCs (two consecutive detections), the PGM-FI warning light will be turned on and DTCs and freeze data will be stored.

blocks.text: *** Procedures for clearing temporary DTCs are described separately.

items.text: The test run shall be performed under the same conditions as the D/C for the initial loss-of-fire detection or the D/C for the determination of no loss-of-fire detection.
80D/C No loss-of-fire detection No loss-of-fire abnormality can be judged as a result of the no loss-of-fire detection judgment.

blocks.text: If the failure diagnosis is determined to be normal by running the vehicle through a series of 3D/C runs under conditions that approximate the conditions under which the vehicle detected the initial failure, the PGM-FI warning light will be turned off. In addition, the PGM-FI warning light, temporary DTC, DTC, and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by disconnecting the 12V battery terminal.
